stories.headline: The Nation: Shadow Play -- Washington's Gears, Grinding; Surprise: Government's Not Necessarily Paralyzed
stories.summary: The US government is still functioning despite the president's personal issues, but these problems are making it hard for the administration to focus on important policy decisions. Some officials are having trouble getting the president's attention due to these distractions.

stories.headline: Sports of The Times: A Sport Is Reborn -- The Home Run, America's Signature Feat, Invigorates Baseball; September's Aura Restored to Fans
stories.summary: The home run record is being challenged by two baseball players, which has brought new excitement to the sport and restored fan interest. This development is being seen as a rebirth for baseball.

stories.headline: THE 1998 CAMPAIGN: The Schumer Record; Schumer Viewed as Relentless, Ambitious Legislator
stories.summary: A US Representative named Charles E Schumer is running in the Democratic primary for the United States Senate, and his campaign is being examined in a series of articles. Schumer is viewed as a driven and ambitious legislator.

stories.headline: Talking the Tawk; New Yorkers Are Sounding More Like Everybody Else. Is It Curtains for the Accent People Love to Hate?
stories.summary: The distinctive New York accent is losing some of its unique characteristics and sounding more like accents from other parts of the country. This change is happening due to increased mobility, immigration, and mass communication, which are reducing regional variations in speech across America.

rounds.blurb: The US government continued functioning despite the president's personal issues, which hindered the administration's focus on policy decisions. Baseball experienced a rebirth as two players challenged the home run record, restoring fan interest. Charles Schumer's legislative record was scrutinized during his Senate campaign.

stories.headline: NEW PUBLICATIONS; THE THIRD PUSEY VOLUME THE LIFE OF EDWARD BOUVERIE PUSEY, D D., Canon of Christ Church; Regius Professor of Hebrew in the University of Oxford. By Henry Parry Liddon, D.D., D.C.L., LL. D. Edited and prepared for publication by the Rev. J.O. Johnston, M.A., and the Rev. Robert J. Wilson, D.D. Vol. III. 1845-1858. New:York: Longmans, Green & Co. $4.50
stories.summary: A new biography about Edward Bouverie Pusey, written by H.P. Liddon, has been published. The book is the third volume in a series about Pusey's life.

stories.headline: NEW THEATRICAL BILLS; Annie Russell Returns to Her First Character, Esmeralda. WILSON BARRETT AS A ROMAN FATHER " The Amazons" in View Again at the Lyceum Theatre and William Collier Has a Brand New Comic Play.
stories.summary: Annie Russell is performing as Esmeralda again, and other new plays are being shown at the Lyceum Theatre.

stories.headline: ARMENIA AND DIPLOMACY; Lord Rosebery Diverts England's Attention and Criticism. FINANCES AND RUSSIAN ALLIANCE LOSS of a liberal Seat at Brigg Will Increase Ministerial Grief at Turkish Crime. ATTITUDE OF TRIPLE ALLIANCE NATIONS Vienna's Official Journals Express Disappointment in Insults -- Berlin Feeling.
stories.summary: There are concerns about England's diplomatic response to issues in Armenia, and the impact of recent election results on the government.

stories.headline: BOND SYNDICATE ENDED; Sold One-fifth of the Fives, but Must Keep, Pro Rata, $19,500,000. MARKET DID NOT EXHIBIT EAGERNESS Bankers Talk of New Threes Against Two-Sevens and of a Better Market for the Fives Shortly.
stories.summary: A bond syndicate has been dissolved, and bankers are discussing the market for new bonds and criticizing the procedure used by Secretary Carlisle.

rounds.blurb: The publication of Henry Parry Liddon's biography of Edward Bouverie Pusey occurred. England's diplomatic response to issues in Armenia was a concern. A bond syndicate was dissolved, affecting the market for new bonds.

stories.headline: SOVIET ADDS SNAGS TO EARLY SOLUTION ON AUSTRIAN PACT; Wants Troops to Stay Pending German Treaty -- Would Curb Vienna Alliances INJECTS TRIESTE ISSUE Molotov Makes Proposal After Figl Urges Quick Settlement -- Dulles Assails New Plan SOVIET ADDS SNAGS TO AUSTRIAN PACT
stories.summary: The Soviet Union is adding conditions to the Austrian peace treaty, including the presence of its troops and limits on Austria's alliances. This move is seen as a complication in the negotiations.

stories.headline: BIG FOUR TO BEGIN TALKS ON AUSTRIA FRIDAY AT LATEST; Ministers Agree in Secret Session to Admit Vienna Aides Into Conference NEWS BLACKOUT IMPOSED Day's Discussions Are Shielded but West Is Said to Offer Limited Far East Parley Big 4 to Begin Austrian Talks By Friday With Viennese Present
stories.summary: The Big Four powers will start talks on Austria, with representatives from Vienna invited to join, and have agreed to keep the discussions private for now.

stories.headline: BRITAIN TO OFFER PACT TO AID E. D. C.; Treaty for Close Army Link Aimed to Overcome French Fear and Spur Approval BRITAIN TO OFFER PACT TO AID E. D. C.
stories.summary: Britain plans to offer a treaty to strengthen its military ties with the European Defense Community in an effort to address French concerns.

stories.headline: PRESIDENT TO ACT IN SLUMP IF MARCH BRINGS NO UPTURN; If Employment Does Not Rise Then, He Tells News Parley, He Will Take Warning TAX RELIEF IS POSSIBLE He Backs Cut in Butter Props -- Goes on 5-Day Vacation in Southern California PRESIDENT LOOKS TO MARCH TRADE
stories.summary: The President has announced that he will take action to address the economic slump if employment does not improve by the end of March, and is considering tax relief measures.

rounds.blurb: The Soviet Union added conditions to the Austrian peace treaty, complicating negotiations. The Big Four powers prepared to begin talks on Austria, inviting Vienna representatives to join. Britain planned to offer a treaty to strengthen its military ties with the European Defense Community.
